MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned by Dept. 2061 for Prove-Up Hearing.
Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703. Email: mlyon@sftc.org.

David Cook, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Andrew Pillet, who was placed under oath and testified.
Michael Berg, Independent CPA and witness for plaintiff, was placed under oath and testified.
Kit Knudson, Esq. appeared for defendant Juniper Twelve, LLC, a suspended California limited liability company.
Having received no objection, the Court GRANTS Mr. Knudson's ex parte application to be relieved as attorney of record for defendant Juniper Twelve, LLC, a suspended California limited liability company.
Proposed Order was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Plaintiff's second amended complaint for Fradulent Conveyance was filed on April 2, 2018.
Order granting defendants Claire Kendrick, Rosemary Kendrick and Katherine Kendrick's motion for summary judgment was filed on March 7, 2019.
Dismissal as to defendant Evergreen Advantage, LLC was filed on September 26, 2016.
DOE defendants were dismissed today, April 15, 2019.

(1) As to the first, second and fourth Causes of Action in the Second Amended Complaint, plaintiff Andrew Pillet shall take nothing from defendant Juniper Twelve, LLC, a suspended California limited liability company; and

(2) Plaintiff's request for Declaratory Relief is DENIED.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Counsel STIPULATES to the return of the exhibits.
